claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have a son named Alex who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me, and I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. So Alex lives with you in Manchester.

Next question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more question: **Are you responsible for Alex's care and upkeep (such as providing food, clothes, and general care for him)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all his needs - food, clothes, school things, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for his care and upkeep
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. 

**What to do next:**
You can make a claim for Child Benefit. You'll need to apply and provide Alex's birth certificate as part of the process.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive Child Benefit, though you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return if your income is above this threshold.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. He lives with you in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the residency requirement. You are responsible for his care and upkeep, providing for all his needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the standard eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for the child's care, and no one else is claiming for this child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 8, who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

You are responsible for his care and upkeep, providing for all his needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the standard eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for the child's care, and no one else is claiming for this child.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 8, who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
